title: OpenAPI 3.2, Compatibility, Deployment, Draining, and Recovery · Inference Brief: Expand Canary Drain Contract
summary: Documentation differs from runtime, examples contain secrets, additive changes break strict clients, storage contracts before old revisions drain, and rollback cannot restore invariants. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts expand canary drain contract increment in versioned API contract and production release defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build once, verify provenance and configuration, expand storage, stage readiness, canary, observe, stop admission, drain HTTP and background work, then contract safely.
Classify additive and breaking changes across fields, nullability, validation, ordering, status, errors, authentication, caching, timing, and business behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Expand Canary Drain Contract · Theory Expand Canary Drain Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 4112 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate one contract-compatible, expand-first, canary, drain, and recoverable release; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then reuse an idempotency key with different input and reject the fingerprint mismatch. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Build once, verify provenance and configuration, expand storage, stage readiness, canary, observe, stop admission, drain HTTP and background work, then contract safely.
